description_long: This is a modern civilian helicopter with a streamlined, aerodynamic fuselage painted primarily in white with black and red accents. The helicopter features a large, rounded cockpit with expansive, dark-tinted windows that wrap around the front and sides, providing excellent visibility for the pilot and passengers. The main rotor consists of four long, slender blades, while the tail section includes a fenestron (enclosed) tail rotor for enhanced safety and noise reduction. The landing gear comprises two sturdy, black skids with crossbars for stability during ground contact. The helicopter's body is constructed from lightweight composite materials and metal, with visible seams and access panels along the fuselage. No visible registration number or alphanumeric markings are present on the exterior.
description: A sleek, white civilian helicopter with a large tinted cockpit, four-blade main rotor, fenestron tail rotor, and black landing skids.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rounded cockpit, four main rotor blades, and the forward curve of the landing skids.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated fuselage, fenestron tail rotor, and the full length of the landing skids.
description_view_2: Rear three-quarter view displaying the tail boom, fenestron tail rotor, and the tapering body shape.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with clear visibility of the cockpit windows, rotor assembly, and landing skids.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four long, slender main rotor blades, likely made of composite material, colored light gray with no visible markings. The blades are evenly spaced and appear to be of equal length, extending well beyond the width of the fuselage.
attribute_tail_rotor: Fenestron (enclosed) tail rotor with multiple small blades arranged within a circular housing at the end of the tail boom. The housing is white, matching the fuselage, and provides additional safety and noise reduction.
attribute_landing_skids: Two black, tubular landing skids with a slight upward curve at the front, connected by crossbars for stability. The skids are made of metal or composite material and are designed for durability and shock absorption.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Large, wraparound cockpit windows with a dark tint, covering the front and both sides of the cockpit. The windows are framed in black and provide wide visibility for the crew.
